topic: AWS ElastiCache-for-Valkey/Redis IAM authentication — end-to-end protocol details for implementing a client-side token provider

sources.title: AWS ElastiCache IAM Authentication (Redis OSS)
keyInsights: Token is presigned URL generated via AWS SigV4
Token passed as password parameter in AUTH/HELLO

sources.title: AWS ElastiCache IAM Authentication (General)
keyInsights: Token construction identical for Redis and Valkey
IAM policy requires both cache and user ARNs

sources.title: ElastiCache RBAC and User Management
keyInsights: User groups required for IAM users
User name and user ID must be identical for IAM auth
Max 100 users per user group, 1000 total users

sources.title: ElastiCache CreateUser API Reference
keyInsights: UserId and UserName both required, must match for IAM

sources.title: AuthenticationMode Data Structure
keyInsights: Passwords array only used when Type=password
Both Type and Passwords are optional fields

sources.title: AWS Service Authorization Reference - ElastiCache
keyInsights: User resource ARN required, cache ARN optional

sources.title: AWS Signature Version 4 Signing Examples
keyInsights: Canonical query string requires alphabetical sorting
Presigned URL includes X-Amz-Algorithm, X-Amz-Credential, X-Amz-Date, X-Amz-SignedHeaders, X-Amz-Signature
Signing key derived from date, region, service, and 'aws4_request'

sources.title: ElastiCache IAM Auth Demo App Documentation
keyInsights: EC2 and ElastiCache must be in same VPC
Uses Lettuce Redis client with IAM token provider

sources.title: Boto3 ElastiCache create_user Documentation
keyInsights: AuthenticationMode dict structure with Type and Passwords

sources.title: AWS CLI create-user Command Reference
keyInsights: No IAM-specific example provided in docs

sources.title: ElastiCache Cluster Creation Guide
keyInsights: User groups assigned during cluster creation
Cluster mode enabled uses CreateReplicationGroup API
Cluster mode disabled uses CreateCacheCluster API
Valkey 7.2+ or Redis OSS 6.0+ required for RBAC

sources.title: ElastiCache Components and Endpoints
keyInsights: Primary endpoint always resolves to current primary node
Reader endpoint round-robins across replicas via DNS
Configuration endpoint for cluster mode discovery
Node endpoints change during topology updates
Auto Discovery for dynamic node detection

sources.title: ElastiCache Replication Group Endpoints
keyInsights: Primary endpoint immune to cluster changes
Reader endpoint keeps up with cluster changes in real-time
Node endpoints not immune to cluster topology changes
Configuration endpoint knows all primary and node endpoints

sources.title: Valkey HELLO Command Specification
keyInsights: Default user works with requirepass mechanism
Simple error reply if protover doesn't exist

sources.title: Lettuce GitHub Issues - ElastiCache
keyInsights: Connection stability issues in AWS ElastiCache
Connection failures after initial successful period

sources.title: Jedis GitHub Repository
keyInsights: No explicit ElastiCache IAM support mentioned

gaps: Exact error codes for expired token not documented
No mainstream Redis client has first-class ElastiCache IAM support
Cluster-mode token reuse across nodes needs empirical verification

notesForImplementers: Token generation requires full AWS SigV4 implementation or AWS SDK dependency
No ElastiCache-specific error codes; must detect expiry via generic AUTH failures
12-hour connection limit requires either reconnect or in-place re-authentication strategy
User-id/user-name identity constraint for IAM users is critical (CreateUser will fail if different)
TLS is non-negotiable; ElastiCache will reject IAM auth attempts without TLS
Token is region-locked; multi-region deployments need per-region token generation
Case normalization: cluster IDs are lowercased by AWS; sign with lowercase to avoid mismatch
